solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int minChanges(int n, int k) {
        if (n == k) return 0;
        int changes = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < 32; ++i) {
            bool n_bit = (n >> i) & 1;
            bool k_bit = (k >> i) & 1;
            if (k_bit == 1 && n_bit == 0) return -1;
            if (n_bit == 1 && k_bit == 0) ++changes;
        }
        return changes;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution compares the binary representations of `n` and `k` bit by bit. If `k` has a bit set to 1 where `n` has a 0, it is impossible to convert `n` to `k`, so we return -1. Otherwise, we count the number of 1s in `n` that need to be flipped to 0 to match `k`. This approach is efficient as it only iterates over a fixed number of bits (32).
